message:
chore(beads): sync issues.jsonl with latest review and fuzz backlog

Flush beads state captured during today's review-only cod pass and the
fuzz-expansion spike:

- New fuzz tracking issues for the DAG and bipartite targets just
  added (franken_networkx-2uup), plus placeholder issues for further
  fuzz targets still to be written (e.g. franken_networkx-l1bn for
  clique enumeration).
- Review-harness gap issues filed as a batch (franken_networkx-2v9o,
  -39gl, -4dzn, -clre) covering random_kernel_graph parity, backend
  keyword signature drift, rescale_layout invalid-input error
  contract, and create_using descriptor errors.
- Category-level conformance harness issues for flow (-59am),
  shortest_path (-dyw3), centrality (-mfgh), and community (-my5o),
  all requesting differential Python harnesses against upstream
  NetworkX with coverage matrices in fnx-conformance.
- Conformance gate drift pinned: asupersync capability matrix gate
  (-miej, P1) still encodes 0.2.0 while fnx-runtime now pins 0.3.1.
- Closures recorded for items already shipped: NodeView mapping
  helpers (-41mz, fixed in 31316f5), multigraph 2-tuple containment
  (-ebkj), and the bipartite.color mock-free integration test
  (-f5vo).

Also refreshes updated_at timestamps on the handful of open parity
tickets touched during triage (EdgeView mapping protocol, adj mapping
helpers, rescale_layout array inputs, random_kernel_graph signature).

No code changes; mirrors `br sync --flush-only` output so state
matches SQLite and .beads/ acts as the git-visible source of truth.
